justification: All 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented via VALID_TRANSITIONS lookup table. Error thrown as InvalidTransitionError class with readonly code property set to 'INVALID_TRANSITION'. Both terminal states (DELIVERED, CANCELLED) enforced via empty sets — no outgoing transitions. 24 tests cover all valid transitions, multiple invalid transitions (6 invalid-transition tests in AC2 alone, plus 5 each for AC3/AC4 terminal states), and AC6 (SHIPPED->PENDING) is explicitly tested in its own describe block. No external libraries used. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs with status 'implemented'.

observations.text: Empty-set pattern for terminal states is elegant — prevents the common failure mode of forgetting to add CANCELLED to the state map
Exhaustive terminal state testing (5 tests each for CANCELLED and DELIVERED, one per possible target) goes beyond minimum requirements
Error shape verified via instanceof check and code property, not just message string matching
AC6 covered both in AC2 suite and in its own dedicated describe block, ensuring explicit coverage
O(1) set-membership check is a clean implementation choice over switch/case chains
